theme: Turn every empty corner into a conversation — the dead floor, the dark monitor, the flat log all have stories to tell

ideas.title: Activity Log: Filter by Worker
ideas.description: The activity log already filters by event type (Hire / Completions / Errors) but has NO per-worker filter. Add a second row of chips to the filter bar — one chip per roster member (their emoji + first name). Clicking a chip sets a worker-name filter variable; entries get double-filtered (type AND worker). Clicking the active chip clears it. The roster array is already available in App.jsx; this is ~15 lines of logic and ~3 lines of JSX. WHY: The single most common question when watching this dashboard is 'what did [person] do today?' Right now the answer requires manually scrolling every entry. This makes it a single click.

ideas.title: Conference Table in the Lower Floor
ideas.description: The bottom 30-35% of the office floor is completely empty dead space — only the door sits there. Add a round conference table centered at ~left:40%, top:70%, implemented as a CSS circle (~13% diameter) with 6 SVG chair arcs arranged around it. The table reacts to vibe: 'crushing' — all 6 chairs show colored role-color dots; 'in-flow' — 3-4 chairs occupied; 'slow-day' — one lone chair pulled out, a coffee cup on the surface; 'on-fire' — a red emergency indicator appears, chairs scattered; 'after-hours' — lamp off, one chair spun sideways. WHY: A round conference table is the single biggest visual upgrade available. The lower third has zero story right now. Making it vibe-reactive gives it three dimensions of narrative without adding any data complexity.

ideas.title: Worker SVG Avatar in Dashboard Cards
ideas.description: Dashboard worker cards render a role emoji as the avatar while the Office tab renders the custom CharacterAvatar SVG for the same workers. They look like different products. Fix: move CharacterAvatar into a shared file (src/components/CharacterAvatar.jsx), import in App.jsx, and replace the .worker-avatar div — which currently calls getRoleEmoji() — with CharacterAvatar sized at 40px. All required props are already on the worker object. WHY: Visual identity consistency across tabs = the product feels cohesive. Right now it feels like two UIs bolted together. Luna's crescent moon, Carlos's hat, Maya's gradient hair — these identities should be everywhere, not just in the Office.

ideas.title: Bloberto's Monitor Displays Live Team Stats
ideas.description: The manager desk monitor is just a dark rectangle with a glow — completely inert despite being front-and-center. Add SVG content inside .mgr-desk__monitor showing real data in a pixel-art aesthetic: a 3-bar mini chart (working / idle / error counts, each bar ~8px wide), a blinking 2-letter vibe abbreviation ('CR' crushing, 'FL' flow, 'SD' slow, 'FR' on-fire, 'AH' after-hours), and a tiny pulse line that twitches when error count > 0. All rendered as child SVG elements, sized to fit within the existing element. Updates passively on each data sync. WHY: Bloberto's desk is the visual anchor of the entire office and his monitor literally faces you at all times. Making it a live command display makes him feel like he's actually managing — not just sitting there glowing.

ideas.title: Stand-Up Mode: Collapsible One-Liner per Worker
ideas.description: Add a 'Stand-Up' toggle button in the Dashboard's 'Active Workers' section header. When on, worker cards collapse to a single-line strip: [emoji] [Name] · [task text 35 chars] · [duration] · [progress%]. All cards become a scannable ~200px block instead of a scrolling grid. Include a copy-to-clipboard button that exports all lines as plain text (e.g. 'Maya: Fix login redirect - 45m - 70%'). Toggle state stored in sessionStorage. WHY: This dashboard is currently a monitor; Stand-Up Mode makes it a tool. A PM can open this, hit the button, and read out the whole team status in 10 seconds. It turns a passive viewer into an active participant in the team's workflow.

ideas.title: Live Activity Ticker at Office Floor Bottom
ideas.description: Add a ticker-tape strip (height: 18px) fixed to the bottom edge of the office-floor div, above the door. It continuously scrolls the last 4 activity log entries leftward via CSS keyframes on a wide inner span. Format: '[icon] [name] [message] · [relTime]' repeating. When a new entry arrives, the string regenerates and the animation resets by toggling a key. No interactivity needed — pure CSS animation, pure JSX string assembly. WHY: The office floor's bottom third is dead space. A scrolling ticker transforms the floor edge into a data stream that creates newsroom energy and makes the space feel alive even when everyone is idle.

ideas.title: Per-Worker Ship Streak Badge
ideas.description: The team vibe streak rewards the collective but individual workers have no personal recognition. In WorkerCard, count activityEntries filtered to entries where worker matches, type is 'complete', and timestamp is 'Just now' / 'Earlier today' — activityEntries and getTimeGroup are already available. If count >= 2, render a small badge beside the worker name: fire x2. At 3+: fire fire x3. WHY: Global vibes celebrate the team; individual streaks celebrate the person. This creates a lightweight achievement layer that rewards grinders and gives visitors a 'who's hot today' at a glance — zero backend required.

ideas.title: Double-Click Bloberto: Auto-Generated Strategy Deck
ideas.description: Add onDoubleClick (plus mobile long-press 500ms) to the Bloberto Character. It opens a full-screen modal: 'Q-[current quarter] Velocity Review — auto-generated by Bloberto AI'. Three slides navigable with arrow keys, Escape to close. Slide 1 'Team Velocity Overview': real live numbers (working count, total, vibe label) overlaid on a satirical SVG bar chart with fake axes labeled Synergy, Alignment, Vibes TBD. Slide 2 'Star Player Spotlight': pulls the highest-progress active worker, renders their CharacterAvatar at 80px with a randomly-selected fake title ('Chief Velocity Officer', 'Senior Mover of Needles', 'Principal Loop Closer'). Slide 3 'Strategic Insights': one quote from an extended satire bank plus a randomly-generated action item. WHY: Bloberto has the richest personality in the product but no deep interaction path. A fake strategy deck is both hilarious and gives him a hidden layer that curious users discover and share. It's the feature that turns a passive dashboard into a product people send links to.
